title: Generation of Blastoids
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"38dpd","text":"This protocol is a part of our collection of protocols for culturing human embryos and human stem cell-derived blastoids in an endometrial culture system.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4qacf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ulr8","text":"This step generates blastoids for implantation into the endometrial scaffold system using the hPSCs prepared in the \"Culture of Human Pluripotent Stem Cells in Naïve Conditions\" step. Generally you would choose between these steps and thawing human embryos unless you are doing experiments on blastoids and embryos in parallel. These steps can be replaced with any other method for generating blastoids, you may wish to use your own preferred method for doing so here instead of following our protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eekst","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7oj8n","text":"This protocol should be undertaken once you have assembled the endometrial scaffold and have begun the hormonal stimulation preparing the scaffold for implantation. This is performed in parallel with a portion of the \"Human Embryo or Blastoid Culture in Endometrial Scaffolds\" protocol, it is recommended that you familiarise yourself with this protocol so that you can time the preparation of the blastoids appropriately to line up with when your scaffold will be ready to receive them, note that this takes longer than thawing embryos so you will need to begin this process earlier.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: This protocol begins following the culture of human pluripotent stem cells in naive conditions described in this protocol: Culture of Human Pluripotent Stem Cells in Naïve Conditions by Richard J Acton For the induction of blastoids we used methods from: Yanagida A, Spindlow D, Nichols J, Dattani A, Smith A, Guo G. The structures resulting from this protocol approximate human embryos at day 5 of development.
Dissociate HNES1 into single cells using Accutase . (!! reagent)

Deplete any MEFs (mouse embryonic fibroblasts) in the cell suspension by plating the cells onto a dish pre-coated with 0.4% gelatin , incubating cells for 00:45:00 . This allows attachment of MEFs to the bottom of the dish. Equipment: (!! 0.4% gelatin coated dish)

Resuspend the unattached cells, enriched in human pluripotent stem cells, in N2B27 basal medium supplemented with: 1.5 micromolar (µM) PD035901 (!! reagent) 1 micromolar (µM) A 83-01 Tocris Catalog #2939 (CAS: 909910-43-6) 10 micromolar (µM) Y-27632 Cell Guidance Systems Catalog #SM02-100 (CAS: 146986-50-7) and distribute the cells so that 120,000 viable cells are seeded into each well of a 24-well AggreWell 400 plate . Each well contains a final volume of 2 mL Equipment: 24-well AggreWell 400 plate AggreWell™400 (Microwell culture plate, Stemcell Technologies, 34411, https://www.stemcell.com/products/aggrewell400.html , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/nqxpbwv5f.webp , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/nqxnbwv5f.webp )
On Day 2, after a total of 48:00:00 , replace the medium with fresh N2B27 basal medium containing 0.5 micromolar (µM) A 83-01 Tocris Catalog #2939 (CAS: 909910-43-6) (half medium change).
Culture for a further 24:00:00 . On day 3, after a total of 72h, replace the medium with N2B27 basal medium .
Stop culture after a further 24:00:00 . On Day 4, after a total of 96h, the blastoids are fully formed.
creator: Richard J Acton
